node.title: Uber | Phone | Find the top k best selling food for each restaurant

topic.title: Uber | Phone | Find the top k best selling food for each restaurant

post.content:
Question: Imagine you are UBER EAT and you have to give top k best selling food report.
Find the top k best selling food for each restaraunt.
Input#1;
restaraunt_name, food_name
"RestaurantA", "apple"
"RestaurantA", "banana"
"RestaurantA", "apple"
"RestaurantB", "coke"
"RestaurantB", "coffee"
"RestaurantB", "coffee"
"RestaurantA", "banana"
"RestaurantA", "orange"

input#2 int k

k = 2
output:
RestaurantA => ["apple", "banana"]
RestaurantB => ["coke", "coffee"]

k = 3 
output:
RestaurantA => ["apple", "banana", "orange"]
RestaurantB => ["coke", "coffee"]

arr = [["RestaurantA", "apple" ], 
["RestaurantA", "banana"],
["RestaurantA", "apple"],
["RestaurantB", "coke"],
["RestaurantB", "coffee"],
["RestaurantB", "coffee"],
["RestaurantA", "banana"],
["RestaurantA", "banana"],
["RestaurantA", "orange"]]

def getCombinations(key,arr):
    dic = defaultdict(int)
    
    [#create](https://leetcode.com/problems/create-maximum-number) dictionary with combination count
    for i,v in arr:
        dic[(i,v)] += 1
    
    [#create](https://leetcode.com/problems/create-maximum-number) final dic as dic["restaurentA"] = heapq(values), push into dictionary values with heapq
    parentdict = defaultdict(list)
    for i,v in dic.keys():
        
        heapq.heappush(parentdict[i],[dic[(i,v)],v])
    # get k largest. 
    return heapq.nlargest(2,parentdict[key])


getCombinations("RestaurantA",arr)
OutPut:- [[3, 'banana'], [2, 'apple']]
Sort by frequency using heap sort and pick top K elements.
